settings.title: Settings
settings.accentColorsDescription: Primary and secondary accent colors used for highlights and indicators.
settings.defaultViewDescription: Article list layout shown by default when opening the app.
settings.readerWidthDescription: Maximum width of article content in the reader pane.
settings.readerFontSizeDescription: Text size in the article reader pane.
settings.defaultSortDescription: Default article sort order in all feeds and categories.
settings.markAsReadDescription: How long after opening an article it gets marked as read. 'Off' disables auto-mark.
settings.markAsReadOnScrollDescription: Articles are automatically marked as read once they scroll out of view.
settings.openOriginalDescription: Open original article in new tab when selecting from list.
settings.hideDuplicatesDescription: When the same article appears in multiple feeds, show it only once (from the first feed that synced it).
settings.rtlLayoutDescription: Mirror the entire interface for Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, and other RTL scripts.
settings.hideEmptyFeedsDescription: Hide feeds and categories with no unread articles from the sidebar.
settings.hideEmptyLabelsDescription: Hide labels with no unread articles from the sidebar.

twoFactor.title: Two-factor authentication
twoFactor.description: Protect your email/password login with a 6-digit authenticator code. OAuth providers like Authelia can enforce MFA separately.
twoFactor.greaderWarning: Google Reader password login is disabled for accounts with 2FA enabled. For SSO flows like Authelia, MFA should be handled by your identity provider.
twoFactor.addToAuthApp: 1. Add this account in your authenticator app
twoFactor.manualSetupHint: Use manual setup if your app does not support opening otpauth links directly.

digest.title: Email Digest
digest.description: Receive a periodic email summary of articles from your feeds. Requires SMTP to be configured by your administrator.
digest.testDigestHint: Sends to your account email using the last 7 days of articles.

push.title: Browser notifications
push.description: Get notified when new articles arrive. Titles are included in notifications.
push.testBody: This is a test from your FeedFerret instance. Notifications are working correctly.

apiTokens.title: API Tokens
apiTokens.description: Personal API tokens for browser extensions, mobile apps, and external integrations. Each token has its own scope and optional expiry. Treat them like passwords.
apiTokens.newTokenWarning: New token — copy now, won't be shown again
apiTokens.name: Name
apiTokens.noTokens: No tokens yet. Create one to get started.
apiTokens.usageHint: Pass the token as Authorization: Bearer <token>. The Fever API uses md5(email:token) as its api_key.

toasts.created: Token created — copy it now, it won't be shown again

ai.title: AI Summaries (BYOK)
ai.description: Summarize articles on demand or automatically on sync using your own OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, OpenRouter, or Ollama credentials. Your API key is encrypted at rest and never shared.
ai.leaveBlankToKeep: Leave blank to keep existing key
ai.autoSummarizeDescription: Automatically summarize new articles (uses API credits)

notifications.title: Notification Channels
notifications.description: Forward keyword alerts and rule matches to external services. Enable a channel, enter your credentials, and choose it as an action in Rules & Alerts.
notifications.chatIdPlaceholder: Chat ID (send /start to your bot to get it)
notifications.telegramHint: Create a bot via @BotFather, then send /start to get your chat ID.
notifications.ntfyHint: Use ntfy.sh for free public topics or your own self-hosted ntfy instance.

syncReaders.title: Sync with external readers
syncReaders.description: Connect Reeder, NetNewsWire, Fluent Reader and other RSS clients to this FeedFerret instance. Hosted services like Feedly need an OPML round-trip instead.
syncReaders.greaderUsernameHint: Username = your FeedFerret email. Password = your FeedFerret password (or an API token).
syncReaders.restApiHint: Used by custom integrations. Authenticate with a bearer token from Profile → API tokens.
syncReaders.twoFactorNoteBody: External clients can't prompt for a TOTP code. If you have 2FA on, generate a dedicated API token under Profile → API tokens and use that in place of your password.

reeder5.notes: Add account → FreshRSS → API URL = your FeedFerret base URL + /api/greader.

netnewswire.notes: Accounts → Add → FreshRSS, then point the API URL at /api/greader.

readkit.notes: Use the FreshRSS option; the Fever variant is not yet exposed.

fluentreader.notes: Add a FreshRSS-style service inside Fluent Reader's service settings.

feedme.notes: Use the “Google Reader API compatible” provider and your /api/greader URL.

newsplus.notes: Pick the GReader/FreshRSS plugin and point it at /api/greader.

feedly.notes: Feedly does not connect to self-hosted servers. Export OPML from Feedly and import it via FeedFerret → Manage feeds → Import/Export.

inoreader.notes: Inoreader is a hosted reader and does not log into FeedFerret. Move feeds via OPML.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Account
deleteAccount.description: Permanently delete your account and all associated data — feeds, articles, labels, and settings. This action is irreversible and complies with GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure).
deleteAccount.confirmDialogDescription: This will permanently delete your account, all your feeds, articles, labels, and settings.
deleteAccount.typeToConfirm: Type delete my account to confirm:

pwa.title: Add to Home Screen
pwa.description: Show instructions for installing FeedFerret as a PWA on your phone or tablet.
pwa.iphoneInstructions: On iPhone/iPad: tap the Share button, then "Add to Home Screen".
pwa.androidInstructions: On Android: tap the browser menu, then "Add to Home Screen" or "Install app".

keyboard.title: Keyboard shortcuts
keyboard.description: Desktop keyboard shortcuts for fast navigation without a mouse.

errors.magicLinkSent: Magic link sent — check your inbox.

errors.failedToRegister: Failed to register. Please try again.

setup.adminAccountDescription: Create the first administrator account for this FeedFerret instance.
setup.instanceSettingsDescription: Customize the name and public URL of this FeedFerret instance.
setup.emailDeliveryDescription: Configure email delivery to enable magic links, digests, and account notifications.
setup.accessControlDescription: Control who can register and sign in to this instance.
setup.allowPublicRegistrationDescription: Anyone with the URL can create an account.
setup.twoFactorNote: Two-factor authentication can be configured after setup in Settings.
setup.quickStartDescription: Add a starter pack of popular feeds to get started quickly.
setup.packsSelected: {count, plural, one {# pack selected} other {# packs selected}}
setup.feedsImported: {count, plural, one {# feed imported} other {# feeds imported}}

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.accountCreationFailed: Account creation failed. Please try again.
errors.signInFailed: Sign-in after setup failed. Please sign in manually.
errors.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection.

sidebar.deleteConfirmDescription: This will permanently remove the feed and all its articles.
sidebar.noNotificationsHint: Keyword alerts and rule matches will appear here.
sidebar.noNotificationsDescription: Set up keyword alerts to get notified when topics you care about appear.

errors.noFeedFound: No feed found at that URL.
errors.discoveryFailedHint: Discovery failed. Try pasting a direct RSS/Atom feed URL.
errors.discoveryFailedToast: Discovery failed — try a direct RSS/Atom URL
errors.imported: {count, plural, one {# feed imported} other {# feeds imported}}

sidebar.spoilerDescription: These articles have been flagged by your rules as spoilers and are kept out of every other view on purpose. Reveal only when you're ready to see them.

articleList.noArticlesDescription: There are no articles matching your current filters.

articleReader.selectArticleDescription: Choose an article from the list to read it here.
articleReader.aiSummaryHint: AI summaries may not be fully accurate. Always verify important information.
articleReader.noContentDescription: This article has no content. Open the original to read it.

discovery.minCharsToSearch: Type at least 2 characters to search.
discovery.useSearchHint: Use the search bar to find feeds by URL or keyword.
discovery.tryTopics: Try different keywords or browse topics.

feedManagement.title: Manage Feeds
feedManagement.description: Organize your feeds, categories, and data.

feeds.addFirstFeed: Add your first feed to start reading.

categories.createCategory: Create a category above to group your feeds.
categories.leaveEmptyForGlobal: Leave empty to use the global default.

labels.title: Labels
labels.labelDescription: Labels help you organize and filter articles across feeds.

savedSearches.title: Saved Searches
savedSearches.description: Advanced queries pinned to the sidebar.
savedSearches.useSaveQuery: Use the search bar to find articles, then save the query for quick access.

health.title: Feed Health Dashboard
health.description: Sync status, errors, article counts and retention.

importExport.alreadyHaveOpml: Already have an OPML file? Drop it here.
importExport.selectFeedsToExport: Select feeds to export, or leave all unchecked to export everything.
importExport.downloadFeedsJson: Download feeds, labels, saved searches, and auto-read rules as JSON.

rules.title: Rules & Alerts
rules.description: Pattern-match articles and run one or more actions.
rules.rulesExplanation: Rules run after each sync and apply one or more actions to matching articles.
rules.addFirstAction: Add at least one action below.
rules.matchingArticle: {count, plural, one {# matching article} other {# matching articles}}
rules.rulesAutoRun: Rules run automatically after each sync.

alerts.createInAppNotifications: Create in-app notifications when newly synced articles match a search query.
alerts.sendBrowserPush: Also send browser push notification when available

webhooks.defaultJsonPayload: Leave empty for the default JSON payload.
webhooks.secretUsage: Used to sign the body via X-FeedFerret-Signature

migration.pickReader: Pick the reader you're moving from.
migration.standardOpml: FeedFerret accepts any standard OPML file.

auth.credentialsUsage: Credentials used when fetching this feed's RSS/Atom URL.

behavior.articlesWontAppear: Articles from this feed will not appear in the All Articles view.
behavior.suppressHeroImage: Suppress the hero image in the article reader.

retention.deleteOlderArticles: Delete read, unstarred, unlabelled articles older than this many days.
retention.alwaysKeep: Always keep at least this many articles for the feed.

fetch.overrideSync: Override global sync interval for this feed.
fetch.disableOnlyForSelfSigned: Disable only for self-signed certs on trusted servers

fulltext.fetchesOriginalPage: Fetches original article page after each sync

scout.customHandling: Use these fields when a site needs custom HTML, XML, JSON, or request handling.

users.deleteUserConfirm: This user and all related data will be removed. This cannot be undone.

registrations.registrationsDescription: When disabled, only existing users can log in.
registrations.require2FADescription: Admin actions are blocked unless the admin account has 2FA active.
registrations.publicSavedSearchesDescription: When disabled, all public saved-search share URLs return 404.
registrations.disableRegistrations: Disable registrations after setting up your accounts on a private server.
registrations.defaultUiLanguageDescription: Language shown to unauthenticated visitors and new users who have not set a preference.

email.enableMailService: Enable magic links, digest emails, and welcome messages.
email.storedChangeToUpdate: Stored — enter new value to change

pushNotifications.title: Push notifications
pushNotifications.description: Configure Web Push (VAPID) credentials for browser push notifications.
pushNotifications.activeSubscriptions: {count, plural, one {# active subscription} other {# active subscriptions}} across {users, plural, one {# user} other {# users}}
pushNotifications.generatedKeysNote: These keys are generated on-the-fly and not stored. Paste them into your environment before closing this view.
pushNotifications.badgeDescription: The installed PWA shows the unread count on its app icon when push is configured.

instance.logoDescription: PNG/SVG/JPEG up to 180 KB. Stored in the database for self-hosted persistence.

starterPacks.title: Starter Packs
starterPacks.name: Name
starterPacks.noFeedsYet: No feeds yet. Add at least one feed before enabling this pack.

sync.automaticallyFetch: Automatically fetch new articles for all feeds at a regular interval.
sync.minOneMinute: Minimum 1 minute. Changes take effect on next server restart.
sync.allowInternalIps: Allow feed fetches to private IPs, localhost, and internal network hosts.
sync.internalUrlWarning: Users can now add feeds that resolve to private/internal network addresses. Only enable on trusted single-tenant deployments.

discovery.title: Feed Discovery Catalog
discovery.description: Import feeds from public directories to enable topic-based feed discovery.
discovery.feedsImportedLocally: Feeds are imported once and stored locally.

storage.title: Storage Usage per User
storage.description: Overview of articles, feeds, and AI summaries per user. Use this to identify heavy users and plan quota policies.

toast.opmlImported: Imported {count, plural, one {# feed} other {# feeds}} into starter pack
toast.logoTooLarge: Logo must be smaller than 180 KB
toast.discoveryImported: Imported {count, plural, one {# new feed} other {# new feeds}}

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.commitment: FeedFerret is committed to making its interface accessible to everyone. Our target is WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance.

features.title: Features
features.skipLink: Skip link: A "Skip to content" link appears at the top of every page when focused.
features.ariaLandmarks: ARIA landmarks: The sidebar uses role="navigation", the article list and reader use role="region" with descriptive labels.
features.keyboardShortcuts: Keyboard shortcuts: All major actions can be performed via keyboard shortcuts. Press ? to see the full list.
features.keyboardNavigation: Keyboard navigation in article lists: Article cards are focusable and activatable with Enter or Space.
features.liveRegions: Screen reader live regions: Unread article counts and search result counts are announced via aria-live regions.
features.reducedMotion: Reduced motion: All animations respect the prefers-reduced-motion media query.
features.iconLabels: Icon button labels: All icon-only buttons carry an aria-label.
features.semanticStructure: Semantic article structure: Article headers use proper h1, author uses address, dates use time.
features.darkMode: Dark mode: Full dark-mode support via the system preference or the in-app toggle.
features.focusManagement: Focus management: Dialogs trap focus and restore it to the trigger element on close.
features.toggleState: Toggle state: Toggle buttons use aria-pressed to communicate state to assistive technologies.

knownLimitations.title: Known Limitations
knownLimitations.dragDrop: Drag-and-drop feed reordering provides keyboard sensors but screen-reader announcements during drag operations have not been fully validated.
knownLimitations.contrast: Contrast for some muted-foreground decorative text elements may fall below 4.5:1 at certain accent color configurations.
knownLimitations.zoom: The 200%-zoom layout has not been formally audited on all screen combinations.
knownLimitations.i18n: The application is available in English and German. Further languages can be contributed via the translation files.

ongoingWork.title: Ongoing Work
ongoingWork.description: We are actively working on the following accessibility improvements:
ongoingWork.axeCore: Automated accessibility testing with axe-core in CI
ongoingWork.roving: Roving tabindex for the feed sidebar list

feedback.title: Feedback & Contact
feedback.description: If you encounter any accessibility barriers, please open an issue on GitHub.

technicalSpecs.title: Technical Specifications
technicalSpecs.radixUi: Radix UI — accessible primitives for dialogs, dropdowns, and navigation
technicalSpecs.tailwind: Tailwind CSS — utility-first styling with responsive design
technicalSpecs.eslint: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y — static analysis for accessibility issues

searchResults.title: Search results
searchResults.resultsCount: {count, plural, =0 {No matches} one {# match} other {# matches}}
searchResults.emptyDescription: Try different keywords or operators like author:, intitle:, is:unread.

sharedSearch.title: Shared FeedFerret search
sharedSearch.noResults: This search has no results yet. Check back after the next sync.

emailDigest.articleCount: {count, plural, one {Here is # article from your feeds.} other {Here are # articles from your feeds.}}
emailDigest.footer: You're receiving this because you enabled email digests in FeedFerret.
emailDigest.subject: {count, plural, one {FeedFerret digest — # new article} other {FeedFerret digest — # new articles}}

emailAuth.signInBody: Click the button below to sign in to your FeedFerret account. This link will expire in 24 hours.
emailAuth.signInIgnore: If you didn't request this email, you can safely ignore it.
emailAuth.testBody: If you received this message, your FeedFerret email provider is configured correctly.
